posts.text: Rupee Depreciation, Gap Downs & Sharp Recoveries ; What’s Driving the Market? — As per this Article, Indian Currency is Depreciating for reasons other than the actual Economic State of Affairs. . Indian banks, seeing a gap between the two prices, started buying dollars cheaply in India and selling them at a higher price in the NDF market, pocketing the difference. The problem was that all this dollar-buying onshore wasn’t genuine import demand — it was pure arbitrage, and it was pushing the rupee down in the very market the RBI was trying to stabilise. In short the citizens savings entrusted t…

posts.text: Rupee Depreciation, Gap Downs & Sharp Recoveries ; What’s Driving the Market? — With the rupee continuing to depreciate against the US dollar, how significant is this factor for the broader equity markets? Can currency weakness directly contribute to market volatility, such as sharp gap-down openings? Also, in recent sessions, we often see sudden recoveries after heavy sell-offs. What are the key factors behind these unexpected recoveries—FII/DII activity, global cues, short covering, or something else? Would like to understand how much impact currency movement really has compared to other mar…
